title: Nintendo 64 Platform Stack
body:
# Nintendo 64 Platform Stack

**Platform stack:** `console_n64` · **Era:** 1996–2002 · **Vendor:** Nintendo · **Count:** 8

NEC VR4300 @ 93.75 MHz with Reality Co-Processor (RSP + RDP) — launched **June 1996** in Japan.

## Stack doctrine

This page lists every combinatorics leaf stamped `platform_stack: console_n64` plus companion dies emitted from `field-cpu-companion-seed.json`. Host and guest CPUs matching the stack's `match` rules receive `always_with` linkage at combinatorics build — no manual wiring.

## Manufacturing notes — companion dies

- **Nintendo RCP** (Nintendo, 64-bit) — Reality Co-Processor — RSP+RDP+MI+AI+VI+PI+RI+SI
- **Nintendo RSP** (Nintendo, 64-bit) — Reality Signal Processor — MIPS-like vector ucode
- **Nintendo RDP** (Nintendo, 64-bit) — Reality Display Processor — rasterizer + framebuffer
- **N64 MIPS Interface (MI)** (Nintendo, 64-bit) — CPU↔RCP register bridge — VR4300 glue
- **N64 Audio Interface (AI)** (Nintendo, 64-bit) — DMA audio buffer to DAC — 44.1kHz path
- **N64 Video Interface (VI)** (Nintendo, 64-bit) — CRT timing + gamma — framebuffer scan-out

## Catalog ids

The `chip_ids` array holds all **8** entries for this platform stack, sorted alphabetically. Run `field-ironclad-chips-combinatorics.py combinatronic` for path percents and imprint renders.

*Stack: console_n64 · seed: data/field-cpu-companion-seed.json · citation: ironclad:chips:3*
